meaning: v.驥肴眠謗貞媄,蜀咲ｻ鍋屐
examples: The company has been forced to realign its operations in the area.
During this re-organization, the ice crystals realign in a way that allows them to slide pass each other.
Photographs lead us to rethink, to realign the frame of our understanding.
Work has now been completed to realign and level a dozen collapsed gravestones and control overgrown vegetation.
Make sure you realign your expectations and cut these six phrases from your vocabulary.
